implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: Concrete SQLite-backed implementations of all seven cli_steering Protocols
Public write API: apply_transition, record_pending_build, record_stage, mark_paused
Public read API: read_status, read_history, read_stages, exists_active_build, find_active_or_recent
Group B duplicate refused via DuplicateBuildError
Lint/format clean: ruff + mypy on modified files

completion_promises.criterion_text: All seven Protocol classes are concrete and pass isinstance checks against the runtime_checkable Protocols defined in pipeline/cli_steering.py
completion_promises.evidence: Seven Sqlite*/AsyncTask* classes (SqliteBuildSnapshotReader, SqliteBuildCanceller, SqliteBuildResumer, SqliteStageSkipRecorder, SqlitePauseRejectResolver, AsyncTaskCanceller, AsyncTaskUpdater) implemented in src/forge/lifecycle/persistence.py. Each exposes the methods of its runtime_checkable Protocol from forge.pipeline.cli_steering. TestProtocolImplementations contains 7 isinstance() checks, all passing. Verified again at REPL: all seven return True for isinstance() against the corresponding Protocol.

completion_promises.criterion_text: apply_transition(t: Transition) -> None is the ONLY public method that issues UPDATE builds SET status = ? (verified by static analysis grep)
completion_promises.evidence: The single 'UPDATE builds SET status = ?' SQL site is inside SqliteLifecyclePersistence.apply_transition. mark_paused, SqliteBuildCanceller.mark_cancelled, SqliteBuildResumer.resume_after_skip all compose a Transition via state_machine.transition() and route through apply_transition. Verified by TestSingleStatusWriter.test_single_status_writer_in_src walking src/ for the regex (asserts exactly 1 hit located in lifecycle/persistence.py). Also verified by the existing test_state_machine.py grep test (which now uses --include=*.py to ignore the .pyc cache).

completion_promises.criterion_text: apply_transition rejects raw kwargs — its signature accepts only Transition objects (Pydantic-validated)
completion_promises.evidence: apply_transition(self, transition: Transition) -> None has exactly one parameter named 'transition'. Body raises TypeError if isinstance(transition, Transition) is False. Verified by TestApplyTransitionSignature.test_signature_has_only_transition_param (introspects via inspect.signature) and test_apply_transition_rejects_non_transition (asserts TypeError for dict, str, None inputs).

completion_promises.criterion_text: mark_paused(build_id, request_id) writes pending_approval_request_id atomically with the state transition (single UPDATE statement)
completion_promises.evidence: mark_paused composes a Transition with pending_approval_request_id=request_id via state_machine.transition() and routes it through apply_transition. apply_transition's UPDATE writes status AND pending_approval_request_id in the same SQL statement guarded by BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT. Verified by TestMarkPausedAtomic.test_mark_paused_persists_request_id which asserts both columns post-call.

completion_promises.criterion_text: record_pending_build(payload) writes the build row and translates IntegrityError on the UNIQUE (feature_id, correlation_id) index into a domain-level DuplicateBuildError (Group B duplicate refused)
completion_promises.evidence: record_pending_build wraps INSERT in BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT and converts sqlite3.IntegrityError to DuplicateBuildError(feature_id, correlation_id) with ROLLBACK on failure. Verified by TestRecordPendingBuildDuplicate (3 tests): first record succeeds, duplicate raises DuplicateBuildError with the right attributes, and only one row exists in the table after the failed second insert.

completion_promises.criterion_text: read_status returns active builds (QUEUED, PREPARING, RUNNING, PAUSED, FINALISING) plus the last 5 terminal builds, sorted by queued_at DESC (per API-cli.md §4.2)
completion_promises.evidence: read_status issues two SELECTs (active set, terminal LIMIT 5) and merges sorted DESC. Verified by TestReadStatus across 5 cases: empty, active-only ordering, terminal cap at 5 (seeded 7 cancelled, asserted 5 returned newest-first), active + terminal global DESC interleave, and feature filter narrowing.

completion_promises.criterion_text: read_history(limit=50) returns at most 50 most recent builds; clamps limit to a max of e.g. 1000 to prevent unbounded queries
completion_promises.evidence: read_history clamps via max(0, min(limit, MAX_HISTORY_LIMIT)) where MAX_HISTORY_LIMIT=1000. Default 50. Negative/zero returns []; non-int raises TypeError. Verified by TestReadHistory across 4 cases: default 50 (60 builds → 50 returned newest-first), clamp constant 1000 exposed, zero/negative empty, type guard.

completion_promises.criterion_text: exists_active_build(feature_id) returns True iff any build for that feature is in {QUEUED, PREPARING, RUNNING, PAUSED, FINALISING}
completion_promises.evidence: exists_active_build issues SELECT 1 WHERE feature_id = ? AND status IN (...) against ACTIVE_STATES. Verified by TestExistsActiveBuild: empty case False, QUEUED True, parametrized COMPLETE/FAILED/CANCELLED False, PAUSED True (after driving QUEUED → PREPARING → RUNNING → PAUSED with mark_paused).

completion_promises.criterion_text: All write paths use BEGIN IMMEDIATE to acquire the write lock atomically (per F7 in the review report — avoids busy-retry under concurrent queue commands)
completion_promises.evidence: apply_transition, record_pending_build, and record_stage all wrap their writes in explicit 'BEGIN IMMEDIATE;' / COMMIT (or ROLLBACK on raise) on the autocommit (isolation_level=None) writer connection. Verified by TestBeginImmediate.test_persistence_module_uses_begin_immediate scanning the source for 'BEGIN IMMEDIATE'. Delegating writes (mark_paused, mark_cancelled, resume_after_skip) inherit the guarantee via apply_transition.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All read paths use read_only_connect() from forge.adapters.sqlite.connect
completion_promises.evidence: SqliteLifecyclePersistence._reader() context manager opens a fresh read_only_connect() against self._db_path on every read call. read_status, read_history, read_stages, exists_active_build, find_active_or_recent all flow through it. For in-memory test databases (path empty) it falls back to the writer connection. Verified by TestReadPathsUseReadOnlyConnect.test_reads_against_real_file_open_a_ro_connection which monkeypatches the helper with a spy and asserts all five read methods invoke it against the writer's db file.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests cover each Protocol method against a real in-memory SQLite database
completion_promises.evidence: tests/forge/test_lifecycle_persistence.py has 60 tests across 14 TestXXX classes. Every Protocol method (get_snapshot, mark_cancelled, resume_after_skip, record_skipped, record_skip_refused, resolve_as_reject, cancel_async_task, update_async_task) plus the read API is exercised against a real SQLite database (tmp_path forge.db with apply_at_boot). 60/60 pass; broader 1544-test forge suite also passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Ran the available linters/type-checkers on the modified files and they all pass cleanly: (1) `ruff check src/forge/lifecycle/persistence.py tests/forge/test_lifecycle_persistence.py tests/forge/test_state_machine.py` → 'All checks passed!' (zero errors). (2) `mypy --ignore-missing-imports --follow-imports=silent src/forge/lifecycle/persistence.py` → 'Success: no issues found in 1 source file'. Removed two unused imports (Transition, BuildSnapshot) from the test file as flagged by ruff F401 in turn-2 fix-up; re-running ruff after the fix returned a clean check. The project does not declare ruff/black/mypy in pyproject.toml as a configured gate, so the canonical check is the ruff default ruleset (the one bundled with the workspace's ruff 0.15.2 install). Both modified files pass.
